note: Hand-maintained calibration layer for tools/build-comets.mjs. Orbital elements (q/e/i/node/peri/tp_jd/epoch_jd) are fetched from JPL SBDB by designation and merged with the h/g here at build time; only photometry (h = absolute total magnitude H, g = activity index n, so apparent mag = h + 5·log10(Δ) + g·2.5·log10(r)) is curated by hand, because SBDB's M1/K1 are unreliable near perigee and must be back-solved from observed peaks (aerith.net / COBS). A periodic comet whose SBDB tp falls outside the build window is advanced by whole orbital periods to its first apparition inside it.

comets.name: Tsuchinshan-ATLAS
comets.note: h/g calibrated to Oct 2024 perigee (delta~0.47 AU, r~0.52 AU, observed ~mag 2)

comets.name: ATLAS
comets.note: near-sungrazer (q=0.093 AU), disintegrated Jan 2025; SBDB K1=12.5 unreliable, h/g estimated

comets.name: Pons-Brooks
comets.note: h/g calibrated to 2024 perihelion (~mag 4.5)

comets.name: Encke
comets.note: h calibrated to ~mag 5 at best geometry (Encke 2003 apparition)

comets.name: Wirtanen
comets.note: h calibrated to 2018 perigee (delta=0.077 AU, r=1.055 AU, observed ~mag 3.9)

comets.name: Schwassmann-Wachmann 1
comets.note: q=5.78 AU (Jupiter-family), next perihelion ~2034 outside window; always faint

comets.name: Olbers
comets.note: h/g calibrated to 2024 perihelion (~mag 7, r=1.17 AU, delta~1.0 AU)

comets.name: ATLAS
comets.note: Kreutz sungrazer (q=0.008 AU), disintegrated Oct 2024

comets.name: Halley
comets.note: next perihelion ~2061, outside window

comets.name: d'Arrest
comets.note: h calibrated to ~mag 8.5 at perihelion

comets.name: Tuttle-Giacobini-Kresak
comets.note: h calibrated to quiescent baseline (~mag 10); known for unpredictable outbursts

comets.name: Kopff
comets.note: h calibrated to ~mag 9.5 at perihelion

comets.name: Honda-Mrkos-Pajdusakova
comets.note: h calibrated to 2017 perigee (delta=0.085 AU, ~mag 7)

comets.name: Lemmon
comets.note: 2026 addition; peaked ~mag 4 Oct-Nov 2025 (perihelion 2025-11-08, r=0.53 AU), fading through early 2026; h back-solved from observed peak

comets.name: SWAN
comets.note: 2026 addition; perihelion 2025-09, close Earth pass 0.26 AU 2025-10 (~mag 5.5); h back-solved from observed peak

comets.name: Tempel 2
comets.note: 2026 addition; Jupiter-family, perihelion 2026-08 (~mag 8-9, telescopic); h calibrated to recent apparition baseline

comets.name: LINEAR
comets.note: naked-eye bright comet 2000; peaked ~mag 6 late Jul 2000 before fragmenting; h back-solved from observed peak

comets.name: LINEAR
comets.note: naked-eye bright comet 2001; the primary fragmented, so SBDB carries the principal fragment A2-A; brightened via fragmentation to ~mag 3.3 May 2001 (q=0.779 AU); h back-solved from observed peak

comets.name: Ikeya-Zhang
comets.note: naked-eye bright comet 2002; peaked ~mag 3.5 late Mar 2002 (q=0.507 AU); h back-solved from observed peak

comets.name: Machholz
comets.note: naked-eye bright comet 2005; peaked ~mag 3.5 early Jan 2005 (close Earth pass 0.35 AU); h back-solved from observed peak

comets.name: McNaught
comets.note: Great Comet of 2007; peaked ~mag −5.5 mid-Jan 2007 but at very low solar elongation (daytime), so the observable events are the dusk-visible ~mag 1 moments the elongation gate admits; q=0.171 AU

comets.name: Lulin
comets.note: naked-eye bright comet 2009; peaked ~mag 5 late Feb 2009 (close Earth pass 0.41 AU); h back-solved from observed peak

comets.name: PANSTARRS
comets.note: naked-eye bright comet 2013; peaked ~mag 1 mid-Mar 2013 (q=0.302 AU); h back-solved from observed peak

comets.name: Lovejoy
comets.note: naked-eye bright comet 2013; peaked ~mag 4.5 late Nov 2013 (close Earth pass 0.40 AU); h back-solved from observed peak

comets.name: Lovejoy
comets.note: naked-eye bright comet 2015; peaked ~mag 3.8 early Jan 2015 (close Earth pass 0.47 AU); h back-solved from observed peak

comets.name: Catalina
comets.note: naked-eye bright comet 2015-16; peaked ~mag 6 around perihelion/perigee late 2015; h back-solved from observed peak

comets.name: NEOWISE
comets.note: Great Comet of 2020; peaked ~mag 0.9 mid-Jul 2020 (q=0.295 AU, close Earth pass 0.69 AU); h back-solved from observed peak

comets.name: Leonard
comets.note: naked-eye bright comet 2021; peaked ~mag 2.6 mid-Dec 2021 (close Earth pass 0.23 AU); h back-solved from observed peak

comets.name: ZTF
comets.note: naked-eye bright comet 2023; peaked ~mag 4.6 early Feb 2023 (close Earth pass 0.28 AU); h back-solved from observed peak
